maxContractTerm: No statutory maximum found for student transportation services contracts. Indiana's school-transportation statute, IC 20-27-5 (Transportation Contracts), contains no cap on the duration of a transportation contract or fleet contract between a school corporation and a private school-bus contractor. IC 20-27-5-7 (verbatim, official text): "Transportation and fleet contracts entered into by a school corporation shall be entered into under IC 5-22." The former specifications/bidding sections in IC 20-27-5 (sections 8 through 9, 11 through 12, 14 through 18, 20, 22, 24 through 25) are marked Repealed in the current code (IDOE Office of School Transportation, Statutes-for-Preservice-July-1-2025, verified via direct PDF text extraction 2026-06-23). The cross-applicable Indiana Public Purchasing Act provides at IC 5-22-17-3(b) (verbatim): "Subject to subsections (c) through (e) and section 5 of this chapter, a contract for supplies may be entered into for a period not to exceed four (4) years." — but by its own terms applies only to contracts for SUPPLIES, not services. Student transportation is a service; IC 5-22-6-1 (verbatim): "The purchasing agency of a governmental body may purchase services using any procedure the governmental body or the purchasing agency of the governmental body considers appropriate," and IC 5-22-6-2 permits the governmental body and its purchasing agency to adopt their own rules and policies. Indiana law therefore imposes no statutory ceiling on the term of a student-transportation services contract; the term is set by local-school-corporation contract specification, subject to the cross-cutting IC 5-22-17-5 cancellation-for-non-appropriation rule and the IC 5-22-17-4 renewal limits.
rebidRequired: Conditional. Indiana law does NOT require a school corporation to run a sealed-bid or other competitive process at the expiration of a student-transportation services contract before retaining or re-awarding the contract; whether to rebid is, as a matter of state statute, a local choice governed by the IC 5-22 procurement framework the local school corporation adopts under IC 5-22-6-1 and the renewal rule in IC 5-22-17-4. The conditional elements: (1) IC 20-27-5-7 routes every transportation and fleet contract into IC 5-22; (2) IC 5-22-6-1 lets a governmental body purchase services "using any procedure the governmental body or the purchasing agency of the governmental body considers appropriate," so services need not be procured by sealed bidding; (3) IC 20-27-5-10(a) requires the governing body to "give notice to the public at least ten (10) days before beginning negotiations or receiving proposals or bids for transportation or fleet contracts" (notice given under IC 5-3-1) — a procedural notice requirement, not a mandate to rebid at term-end; (4) IC 20-27-5-10(b): "A transportation or fleet contract may not be negotiated until notice has been given under this section"; (5) IC 5-22-17-4(b): "Subject to section 5 of this chapter, with the agreement of the contractor and the purchasing agency, a contract may be renewed any number of times," and (c) caps each renewal at the original-contract term. Together these mean a school corporation may, at term-end, either re-solicit under IC 5-22 (publishing the IC 5-3-1 / IC 20-27-5-10 notice) or mutually renew under IC 5-22-17-4 without a fresh competitive solicitation. If the corporation chooses to rebid, IC 20-27-5-13 still imposes prerequisites on bidders (physical examination certificate and valid public passenger chauffeur's/CDL license).
renewalMechanics:
Renewal of a student-transportation services contract is governed by IC 5-22-17-4 (Renewal of contracts), made applicable through IC 20-27-5-7. Three operative rules (verified verbatim against the codified chapter PDF):

(a) IC 5-22-17-4(a): "A contract that contains a provision for escalation of the price of the contract may be renewed under this section if the price escalation is computed using: (1) a commonly accepted index named in the contract; or (2) a formula set forth in the contract." — Authorizes CPI-style indices, named-formula escalators, and (by ordinary contract drafting) fuel escalators tied to a published index.

(b) IC 5-22-17-4(b): "Subject to section 5 of this chapter, with the agreement of the contractor and the purchasing agency, a contract may be renewed any number of times." — Renewal is by mutual written agreement; there is no statutory cap on the number of renewals.

(c) IC 5-22-17-4(c): "The term of a renewed contract may not be longer than the term of the original contract." — Each renewal capped at the duration of the underlying original award.

Indiana statute does NOT provide for automatic / evergreen / rollover renewal — the text requires "agreement of the contractor and the purchasing agency," i.e., affirmative bilateral consent each time. State-agency approval is not required for term-end renewal; charter-school / IDOE prior approval is not required. Board approval is required only because IC 20-27-5-5(a) and IC 20-27-5-6(a) treat the school board as the contracting authority. All renewals remain subject to IC 5-22-17-5(a): "When the fiscal body of the governmental body makes a written determination that funds are not appropriated or otherwise available to support continuation of performance of a contract, the contract is considered canceled." Sale or assignment of the contract requires written agreement of both original parties and the assignee/purchaser under IC 20-27-5-19.
statutoryCitation:
Primary transportation authority: Indiana Code Title 20 (Education), Article 27 (School Transportation), Chapter 5 (Transportation Contracts) — particularly IC 20-27-5-5 (transportation contract; school bus driver provides body/chassis; "the governing body of the school corporation may enter into a transportation contract with the school bus driver under IC 5-22"); IC 20-27-5-6 (fleet contract; "the governing body of the school corporation may enter into a fleet contract with the fleet contractor under IC 5-22"); IC 20-27-5-7 (public purchasing requirements; "Transportation and fleet contracts entered into by a school corporation shall be entered into under IC 5-22"); IC 20-27-5-10 (public-notice of at least 10 days under IC 5-3-1); IC 20-27-5-13 (bidder prerequisites: physical exam + valid public passenger chauffeur's or CDL license); IC 20-27-5-19 (sale/assignment requires written agreement of both original parties and assignee/purchaser); IC 20-27-5-23 (termination without hearing for alcohol violation); IC 20-27-5-27 (governing body may purchase contractor's equipment at fair market value on cancellation); IC 20-27-5-28 (surety bond on faithful performance). Sections IC 20-27-5-8 through 9, 11 through 12, 14 through 18, 20, 22, 24 through 25 are Repealed.

Cross-applicable procurement authority: IC 5-22 (Public Purchasing) — IC 5-22-2-13 ("Governmental body" includes "another establishment of … (4) A political subdivision"); IC 5-22-6-1 (services may be purchased "using any procedure the governmental body or the purchasing agency of the governmental body considers appropriate"); IC 5-22-6-2 (rules/policies); IC 5-22-17-3 (4-year cap on contracts for SUPPLIES only); IC 5-22-17-4 (renewal rules — CPI/formula escalation, mutual agreement, renewal term ≤ original term); IC 5-22-17-5 (cancellation on non-appropriation).

General authority of school corporation governing body to provide/contract for transportation: IC 20-26-5-4(a)(10) ("Subject to IC 20-27-13, to transport children to and from school, when in the opinion of the governing body the transportation is necessary, including considerations for the safety of the children.").

Official URLs: Indiana Department of Education, Office of School Transportation, "School Transportation Statutes — Updated July 1, 2025" (https://www.in.gov/doe/files/Statutes-for-Preservice-July-1-2025.pdf); IC 5-22 chapter PDFs from statecodesfiles.justia.com mirror (chapter-2/-6/-17) — verified verbatim text matches "Indiana Code 2016" official codification; SBOA Schools portal cross-references (https://www.in.gov/sboa/political-subdivisions/schools/) explicitly listing "Time period for contracts - generally 4 years, IC 5-22-17-3" and "Renewal of contracts, IC 5-22-17-4" as operative for school corporations.
districtVariance: Unknown. No Indiana Attorney General opinion located that interprets IC 20-27-5 or IC 5-22-17 specifically with respect to the term, rebid, or renewal of a student-transportation services contract. SBOA Schools guidance (https://www.in.gov/sboa/political-subdivisions/schools/) treats IC 5-22-17-3 (4-year supplies cap) and IC 5-22-17-4 (renewal) as the operative cross-references and points the field to IC 5-22 generally, but does not catalog district-by-district practice variations on contract term length or rebid cadence. ISBA Policy Advisor materials cover transportation generally but the publicly indexed materials reviewed do not document an interpretive split. The structural reason variance is plausible: IC 5-22-6-1 expressly delegates service-procurement procedure to the local "governmental body or the purchasing agency of the governmental body," and IC 5-22-6-2 lets each body adopt its own rules/policies; with no statutory ceiling on term and broad renewal latitude under IC 5-22-17-4, contract-length practice is left to local board policy. Without an indexed AG opinion, SBOA bulletin, or statewide compliance audit addressing per-district contract-length variation, the field is recorded as Unknown — credible evidence either way was not located within the search budget despite querying SBOA Schools portal, IDOE Office of School Transportation pages, the Indiana AG official-opinions index (https://www.in.gov/attorneygeneral/about-the-office/advisory/official-opinions-of-the-indiana-attorney-general/), the Indiana Register (iar.iga.in.gov), and ISBA Policy Advisor materials.
specialEducation: No separate Indiana statutory rule was located that imposes a different term limit, rebid requirement, procurement procedure, or renewal mechanic on contracts for special-education transportation, IEP-mandated transportation, McKinney-Vento (homeless) transportation, or foster-care transportation. Indiana's special-education transportation statute, IC 20-35-8-2 (Transportation for individualized education program; rules on limitations; liability for costs; transportation by appropriate vehicle), directs the State Board of Education to adopt rules limiting the AMOUNT of transportation an IEP may require (first/last day, school-holiday round trips, two additional round trips per school year) and allocates COSTS among school corporations and the state; it does not create a separate contracting regime — a school corporation that contracts with a private contractor for IEP-required transportation still proceeds under IC 20-27-5 (and therefore IC 5-22). Homeless-student transportation under IC 20-27-12-4 imposes an inter-corporation cost-sharing obligation (shared-equally default in IC 20-27-12-4(c)) and IC 20-27-12-5 specifies vehicle types (special-purpose bus if more than 7 students; appropriate vehicle if 7 or fewer) — neither creates a separate contracting term/rebid/renewal rule. Foster-care transportation under IC 20-50-3 and the shared homeless/foster-care vehicle rule in IC 20-27-12-5 likewise govern vehicle type, driver qualifications (IC 20-27-9-5(c)), and cost allocation rather than contract term/rebid/renewal. Federal IDEA (20 U.S.C. § 1401(26)) and McKinney-Vento (42 U.S.C. § 11432) impose a federal duty to provide transportation, but those federal duties go to the obligation to transport, not to the state-law term/rebid/renewal rules for the private contracting vehicle — recorded here as background, not as a state contracting rule. Bottom line: the IC 20-27-5 / IC 5-22 framework above governs the contractor relationship for regular routes, special-education routes, IEP-required routes, homeless and foster-care routes, and alternative student-transportation vendors alike. Source: IDOE Office of School Transportation Statutes booklet, July 1, 2025, reproducing IC 20-27, IC 20-35-8, and IC 20-27-12 (verbatim verified 2026-06-23 via direct PDF text extraction).
notesAndSpecialProvisions:
- **Procurement framework is IC 5-22, not IC 20-27.** IC 20-27-5-7 explicitly requires that "Transportation and fleet contracts entered into by a school corporation shall be entered into under IC 5-22." The school-transportation chapter's own former bidding-and-specifications provisions (IC 20-27-5-8 through 9, 11 through 12, 14 through 18, 20, 22, 24 through 25) are now Repealed in the current Indiana Code (verified verbatim against DOE 2025 booklet).
- **Services vs. supplies distinction is decisive.** IC 5-22-17-3 caps SUPPLIES contracts at 4 years (5 for county/municipal-hospital purchases under IC 5-22-7). Student transportation is a SERVICE, not a supply, and falls under IC 5-22-6-1's "any procedure the governmental body … considers appropriate" rule. The 4-year cap does not apply to a transportation services contract on its statutory face.
- **Small-purchase thresholds (general IC 5-22 services framework).** SBOA-published guidance treats IC 5-22 small-purchase thresholds as: below $50,000, small-purchase policies under IC 5-22-8-2; $50,000-$150,000, quotes from at least three vendors; above $150,000, IC 5-22-7 sealed bidding for supplies. These thresholds chiefly drive supplies-procurement; the IC 5-22-6 services rule lets the local body define its own services procedure. (Source: AIM Indiana Public Purchasing Guide and SBOA IASBO Purchasing presentation.)
- **Sole-source / special purchasing methods.** IC 5-22-10 authorizes specific exceptions (single source for supplies — IC 5-22-10-13, emergency, etc.). These are written-determination based and apply chiefly to supplies; their applicability to transportation services is mediated through IC 5-22-6's "any procedure" rule.
- **Cooperative purchasing / piggyback.** A school corporation may enter cooperative-purchasing arrangements under the Interlocal Cooperation Act (IC 36-1-7) and IC 5-22. The Indiana Department of Administration's K12Indiana.com portal provides statewide purchase agreements accessible to school corporations and charter schools.
- **Notice requirement (transportation-specific).** IC 20-27-5-10(a) requires the governing body to give public notice "at least ten (10) days before beginning negotiations or receiving proposals or bids for transportation or fleet contracts" (notice given in the manner of IC 5-3-1); (b) prohibits negotiating a transportation/fleet contract without this notice. This applies to new awards and re-solicitations.
- **Bidder prerequisites.** IC 20-27-5-13 conditions any transportation contract award on the bidder (i) meeting IC 20-27-8-1 physical requirements per a certificate from an Indiana physician, and (ii) holding a valid public passenger chauffeur's license or CDL.
- **Surety bond.** IC 20-27-5-28 requires the contractor to "furnish a surety bond conditioned on faithful performance of the contract"; the governing body sets the amount.
- **Sale or assignment.** IC 20-27-5-19 — "A transportation or fleet contract entered into under this chapter may not be sold or assigned except by written agreement of both parties to the original contract and by the assignee or purchaser of the contract."
- **Equipment buyout on cancellation.** IC 20-27-5-27 — on cancellation, the governing body may purchase the contractor's school-bus equipment at "fair market value" determined by agreement of the parties.
- **Termination for alcohol violation (no hearing).** IC 20-27-5-23 — a transportation contract may be terminated without hearing on reliable evidence of alcohol consumption by the driver during school hours, while operating a school bus, or while performing the school bus driver's duties.
- **Non-appropriation cancellation (IC 5-22-17-5).** Written determination by the fiscal body that funds are not appropriated/available cancels the contract; this determination is "final and conclusive."
- **Charter schools.** Charter schools (IC 20-24) are included within "school corporation" for several enumerated purposes; charter procurement is governed by Indiana Charter School Board procurement policies and the IC 5-22 framework generally. Charter Operations-Fund usage for transportation is governed by IC 20-40-18-10.5.
- **TNC option.** IC 20-27-5-5(c) and 5-6(c) authorize school corporations to enter "transportation agreement[s]" or "fleet agreement[s]" with a Transportation Network Company (TNC, IC 8-2.1-17-18) if expanded criminal-history and CPS-index checks per IC 20-26-5-10 are performed on every TNC driver (IC 8-2.1-17-19) who will transport students.
- **Common-carrier contracts.** IC 20-27-5-29 through 5-32 allow contracts with regular-route common carriers under DOR jurisdiction with their own specifications regime (carrier solely responsible for driver employment/conduct; driver physical certificate required; exempt from certain construction/equipment specs subject to operational conditions).
- **Special-education transportation.** No separate state-law contracting rule (IC 20-35-8-2 governs IEP transportation extent and cost allocation, not contracting term/rebid/renewal).
- **Homeless-student transportation.** No separate contracting rule (IC 20-27-12-4 inter-corporation cost-sharing; IC 20-27-12-5 vehicle types).
- **Foster-care transportation.** No separate contracting rule (IC 20-50-3 cost allocation; IC 20-27-12-5 vehicle types).
- **Township-trustee carve-out (historical residue).** Secondary indexed sources reference a rule that "a township trustee may not enter into a school bus contract that has a term extending beyond the June 30 following the expiration date of the trustee's term of office." Adversarial review: this rule is NOT visible in the current IC 20-27-5 text in the DOE 2025 booklet — direct PDF extraction confirms it does not appear in sections 1 through 33 of the current chapter. It appears to be residual from the pre-2005 IC 20-9.1-2 framework and applied to a township-trustee-as-school-authority structure that no longer exists in current Indiana school governance. Treated as historical/inapplicable; not a generally applicable rule on private contractors today.
- **Renewal cap (IC 5-22-17-4(c)).** Each renewal may not exceed the original term, but unlimited renewals are permitted with mutual agreement — this gives Indiana a de facto "term + unlimited equal-or-shorter renewals" structure rather than a hard ceiling.
- **General governing-body authority.** IC 20-26-5-4(a)(10) — "Subject to IC 20-27-13, to transport children to and from school, when in the opinion of the governing body the transportation is necessary, including considerations for the safety of the children" — is the umbrella discretion grant under which a board contracts (or self-operates).
exceptionsUnresolved:
All five fields are resolved on primary authority. Max Contract Term, Rebid Required, Renewal Mechanics, Statutory Citation, and Special Education are all verified verbatim against (a) the official DOE Office of School Transportation Statutes-for-Preservice-July-1-2025 PDF (downloaded and text-extracted on 2026-06-23, confirming IC 20-27-5-7's "shall be entered into under IC 5-22" language and the Repealed status of sections 8 through 9, 11 through 12, 14 through 18, 20, 22, 24 through 25) and (b) the codified IC 5-22 chapter PDFs (chapters 2, 6, and 17), verified verbatim 2026-06-23.

The single field that remains open is District-Level Interpretation Variance (recorded as "Unknown"). Searched: (a) Indiana Attorney General official-opinions index (https://www.in.gov/attorneygeneral/about-the-office/advisory/official-opinions-of-the-indiana-attorney-general/) — no AG opinion located interpreting IC 20-27-5 or IC 5-22-17 with respect to inter-district variance in transportation-contract term, rebid, or renewal practice; (b) SBOA Schools portal (https://www.in.gov/sboa/political-subdivisions/schools/) — confirms cross-reference framework but does not catalog district-by-district variance in transportation-contract length or rebid cadence; (c) Indiana Register (iar.iga.in.gov) advisory opinions on school transportation — pages indexed but full opinion text not retrievable within the search budget for the JS-rendered register pages; (d) ISBA Policy Advisor materials — covered general transportation policy but no inter-district variance survey located. Reason still open: lack of indexed, authoritative statewide compliance data showing whether Indiana districts uniformly limit themselves to a particular contract length (e.g., 1-year, 3-year, 5-year) or whether term length varies by district. Given that IC 5-22-6-1 expressly delegates services-procurement procedure to the local body, variance is plausible but not affirmatively documented in indexed authority within the search budget.

A secondary item resolved via adversarial review: WebSearch snippets initially surfaced conflicting paraphrases of IC 20-27-5-7 — one set quoted the OLD pre-amendment language ("Transportation or fleet contracts may either be negotiated and let after receiving bids on the basis of specifications…" — visible in 2006/2011/2012 Justia snapshots); the other set quoted the CURRENT language ("Transportation and fleet contracts entered into by a school corporation shall be entered into under IC 5-22"). The conflict was resolved against the OLD snippet by direct verbatim extraction from the official DOE July 1, 2025 booklet, which definitively shows the current text as the IC 5-22 cross-reference, with sections 8 through 18 Repealed. The Justia 2006/2011/2012 snapshots are historical and superseded.

sourceLedgerRows.sourceTier: Tier 1 — State agency reproduction of codified state statute (Indiana Department of Education, Office of School Transportation, compiled current code)

sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term; Rebid Required; Renewal Mechanics; Statutory Citation; Special Education (negative finding); confirms IC 20-27-5-7 routes transportation/fleet contracts to IC 5-22; confirms IC 20-27-5-8 through 9, 11 through 12, 14 through 18, 20, 22, 24 through 25 are Repealed
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: IC 20-27-5-7 verbatim: "Transportation and fleet contracts entered into by a school corporation shall be entered into under IC 5-22." IC 20-27-5-5(a) and 5-6(a): the governing body "may enter into a transportation contract" (or "fleet contract") "under IC 5-22." IC 20-27-5-10(a): governing body shall give public notice "at least ten (10) days before beginning negotiations or receiving proposals or bids." IC 20-27-5-13, 5-19, 5-23, 5-27, 5-28 set ancillary requirements as quoted in notes.

sourceLedgerRows.sourceTier: Tier 1 — Codified state statute (Indiana Public Purchasing Act, official chapter PDF)
sourceLedgerRows.citation: IC 5-22-17 (Contracts), full chapter text — Sec. 3 (Contracts for supplies; time periods); Sec. 4 (Renewal of contracts); Sec. 5 (Funds not appropriated; cancellation)
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Indiana Code 2016 (sections cited added by P.L.49-1997, Sec. 1; Sec. 4 amended by P.L.153-1999, Sec. 5; no subsequent material amendments to sections 3 or 4 located in 2017-2026 session-law sweep; Justia 2024 codified version confirmed unchanged text)
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term (the 4-year cap applies to SUPPLIES only, not services); Renewal Mechanics (CPI/formula escalation, mutual agreement, renewal term ≤ original); cross-reference for Statutory Citation
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Sec. 3(b) verbatim: "Subject to subsections (c) through (e) and section 5 of this chapter, a contract for supplies may be entered into for a period not to exceed four (4) years." Sec. 4(a): renewal where price escalation uses commonly accepted index or formula. Sec. 4(b): "Subject to section 5 of this chapter, with the agreement of the contractor and the purchasing agency, a contract may be renewed any number of times." Sec. 4(c): "The term of a renewed contract may not be longer than the term of the original contract." Sec. 5(a): non-appropriation cancellation; determination "final and conclusive."

sourceLedgerRows.sourceTier: Tier 1 — Codified state statute (Indiana Public Purchasing Act, official chapter PDF)

sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term (services exempt from IC 5-22-17-3 4-year cap because services-procurement procedure is local); Rebid Required (no mandatory sealed-bid for services); Statutory Citation
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Sec. 1 verbatim: "The purchasing agency of a governmental body may purchase services using any procedure the governmental body or the purchasing agency of the governmental body considers appropriate." Sec. 2: governing body and purchasing agency may adopt their own rules and policies.

sourceLedgerRows.sourceTier: Tier 1 — Codified state statute (Indiana Public Purchasing Act, official chapter PDF)

sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Confirms applicability of IC 5-22 to school corporations (political subdivisions); Statutory Citation
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Sec. 13 verbatim: "'Governmental body' means an agency, a board, a branch, a bureau, a commission, a council, a department, an institution, an office, or another establishment of any of the following: (1) The executive branch. (2) The judicial branch. (3) The legislative branch. (4) A political subdivision."

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term: "No statutory maximum found for student transportation services contracts."
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: An IC 20-27-5 section (or any general IC 5-22 services provision) does cap the term of a transportation services contract — searched for 'notwithstanding', 'may renew', 'extension', 'option', 'successive', 'multi-year', 'not exceed', 'annual termination', 'term', 'duration', 'years', 'contract period'.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Full text of IC 20-27-5 extracted from DOE 2025 booklet; verbatim search of IC 5-22-6 and IC 5-22-17 chapter PDFs; WebSearch for term/duration provisions; SBOA Schools portal; SBOA School Bulletin June 2025; AIM 2021 Public Purchasing Guide.
challengeMatrixRows.result: No cap on a transportation SERVICES contract located. The IC 5-22-17-3 4-year cap, on the face of the statute, applies only to contracts for SUPPLIES. IC 5-22-6-1 expressly delegates services-procurement procedure to the local governmental body. SBOA cross-references IC 5-22-17-3 as 'generally 4 years' but the operative statutory limit is the supplies-only language.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Rebid Required: Conditional — no mandate to rebid; renewal permitted under IC 5-22-17-4.
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: A mandatory bidding requirement applies at term-end via either IC 20-27-5 (school transportation), IC 5-22 (procurement), IC 36-1-12 (public works), IC 4-13.6 (state public works), or district-purchasing law.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Full IC 20-27-5 text re-read for any 'shall rebid' or term-end re-solicitation language; IC 5-22-17 chapter for mandatory rebid; WebSearch for 'mandatory bidding' + 'transportation' + 'Indiana school'.
challengeMatrixRows.result: No mandatory rebid located. IC 20-27-5-10 imposes a 10-day public-notice procedural floor when the body chooses to negotiate, receive proposals, or receive bids — it does not mandate that the body choose to do so at term-end. Renewal without re-bid is expressly authorized by IC 5-22-17-4(b).

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics: IC 5-22-17-4 — CPI/formula escalation, mutual agreement, renewal term ≤ original; unlimited renewals.
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Limits/conditions exist (CPI caps, fuel adjustments, board/state approval mandate, annual appropriations rule, termination notice, public-hearing, max number of renewals) that Stage 1 missed.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Verbatim IC 5-22-17-4 text extracted from chapter PDF; checked IC 5-22-17-5 (non-appropriation), IC 5-22-17-3(d) (payment/performance subject to appropriation), IC 5-22-17-7 (hazardous waste, longer term — inapplicable), IC 5-22-17-8 (EBT program, longer term — inapplicable).
challengeMatrixRows.result: IC 5-22-17-4 text is exactly as Stage 1 stated; no cap on number of renewals; renewal term cannot exceed original term; renewal requires mutual agreement; renewal is subject to IC 5-22-17-5 non-appropriation cancellation. No additional limit located.

challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: (a) IC 20-27-5-7 has been amended or replaced and no longer reads as Stage 1 quoted; (b) the cited sections of IC 20-27-5 are themselves Repealed; (c) IC 5-22 chapter PDFs cited are out-of-date and the current sections differ.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Direct PDF download and pdftotext extraction of (i) DOE 2025 booklet for IC 20-27-5 text and Repealed-section list; (ii) chapter-17 PDF of IC 5-22; (iii) chapter-6 PDF; (iv) chapter-2 PDF. Plus WebSearch session-law sweep 2017-2026 for amendments to the cited sections. Cross-checked vLex chapter index for current section names and Repealed status.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Verified verbatim. The DOE 2025 booklet text of IC 20-27-5-7 ('Transportation and fleet contracts entered into by a school corporation shall be entered into under IC 5-22') and the Repealed-section list (8 through 9, 11 through 12, 14 through 18, 20, 22, 24 through 25) match Stage 1's claims. IMPORTANT ADVERSARIAL FINDING: WebSearch initially surfaced conflicting paraphrases of IC 20-27-5-7 quoting the OLD pre-amendment text ('negotiated and let after receiving bids on the basis of specifications'). This older text appears in the 2006/2011/2012 Justia archived Indiana Code snapshots. Confirmed via direct verbatim extraction of the DOE 2025 booklet that the OLD text is superseded — current law is the IC 5-22 cross-reference.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: No change to substantive finding; adversarial process surfaced and resolved a citation-version conflict in Stage 1's favor.

challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: A statewide AG opinion, SBOA bulletin, or compliance-audit dataset documents systematic inter-district variance (or lack thereof) in transportation-contract length and rebid cadence.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Indiana AG official-opinions index re-checked; SBOA Schools portal re-checked; SBOA June 2025 School Bulletin downloaded and read (no transportation-contract-length content); ISBA Policy Advisor index; Indiana Register search.
challengeMatrixRows.result: No statewide compliance dataset or AG opinion on inter-district transportation-contract-length variance located within the search budget. Stage 1's 'Unknown' verdict holds — variance is plausible (because IC 5-22-6-1 delegates services-procurement procedure locally), but not affirmatively documented in authoritative indexed sources.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Special Education: No separate Indiana statutory rule on term/rebid/renewal for special-ed/IEP/McKinney-Vento/foster-care transportation contracting.
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: IC 20-35-8, IC 20-27-12, IC 20-50-3, or IC 511 IAC contains a separate contracting term/rebid/renewal regime for vulnerable-population transportation.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Direct PDF extraction of IC 20-35-8-2 (full text), IC 20-27-12-4 (full text), IC 20-27-12-5 (full text) from DOE 2025 booklet.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed verbatim. IC 20-35-8-2 governs the AMOUNT and COST of IEP transportation (state-board rules + cost allocation between corporations and state); IC 20-27-12-4 imposes inter-corporation cost-sharing for homeless students; IC 20-27-12-5 specifies vehicle types and driver qualifications. None creates a separate contracting term/rebid/renewal rule — the underlying private contract remains under IC 20-27-5 / IC 5-22.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Currentness: code currency = July 1, 2025 for IC 20-27 and 2016/unchanged-through-2025 for IC 5-22-17 sections.
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: A post-July-1-2025 amendment or a session-law amendment to IC 5-22-17-3, 17-4, 17-5, or IC 5-22-6-1 changes the substantive rule.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: WebSearch for 'IC 5-22-17-3 amended 2020-2025'; WebSearch for 'IC 5-22-17-4 2024 2025 renewal'; WebSearch for post-2025 Indiana session laws touching IC 20-27 / IC 5-22-17.
challengeMatrixRows.result: No material amendment located. Justia 2024 codification of IC 5-22-17-4 returns identical text to the 2016 chapter PDF. IC 5-22-17-3 text identical 2022 and 2023 Justia codifications. No 124th General Assembly (2025 / 2026) bill located that amends IC 5-22-17-3, 17-4, or IC 5-22-6-1 substantively.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Township-trustee carve-out (Stage 1 notes: 'a township trustee may not enter into a school bus contract that has a term extending beyond the June 30 following the expiration date of the trustee's term of office').
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: This rule is still active in current IC 20-27-5 and applies to today's private-contractor transportation contracts.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Direct PDF extraction of IC 20-27-5 (sections 1 through 33) from DOE 2025 booklet — searched for 'township', 'trustee', 'June 30', 'term of office'.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Rule NOT present in current IC 20-27-5 text. It is residual from the pre-2005 IC 20-9.1-2 framework when township trustees served as school authorities for some districts. Stage 1's treatment as historical/inapplicable is confirmed and tightened in the reconciled notes.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: No change to the five core fields; Stage 1 already correctly flagged it as historical.

perFieldVerdicts.note: Verified verbatim via direct PDF text extraction of the IDOE July 1, 2025 booklet (IC 20-27-5-7 routes to IC 5-22; sections 8 through 18 marked Repealed) and the IC 5-22-17-3 chapter PDF ('contract for supplies may be entered into for a period not to exceed four (4) years' applies only to supplies).

Verified verbatim — IC 20-27-5-10 imposes only a 10-day procedural notice; IC 5-22-6-1 lets the body use 'any procedure … considers appropriate' for services; IC 5-22-17-4(b) authorizes renewal 'any number of times' with mutual agreement.
Verified verbatim — IC 5-22-17-4(a)/(b)/(c) text confirmed via direct PDF extraction of the chapter PDF; matches Stage 1 quotation exactly. No additional limits or carve-outs surfaced.

All cited sections re-opened on the official DOE 2025 booklet and the IC 5-22 chapter PDFs and confirmed verbatim. Adversarial process resolved a citation-version conflict (older Justia snapshots from 2006/2011/2012 quote a different, superseded text of IC 20-27-5-7) in favor of Stage 1's current-law citation.

Stage 1's 'Unknown' verdict holds. No AG opinion, SBOA bulletin, or statewide compliance dataset documenting inter-district variation in transportation-contract length or rebid cadence was located within the search budget. The 'unresolved' label here marks Stage 1's documented inability to confirm or refute — not an adversarial dispute. (Default to confirmed-unknown.)

Verified verbatim via direct PDF extraction of IC 20-35-8-2, IC 20-27-12-4, IC 20-27-12-5 from the DOE 2025 booklet. No separate contracting term/rebid/renewal rule for IEP, homeless, or foster-care transportation.

verificationNotes:
**SOURCE-VALIDATION REVISIT (load-bearing primary citations).**

(1) IDOE Office of School Transportation, Statutes-for-Preservice-July-1-2025 PDF — re-opened on 2026-06-23 by direct curl download (https://www.in.gov/doe/files/Statutes-for-Preservice-July-1-2025.pdf, 484KB) and pdftotext extraction. Confirmed VERBATIM: IC 20-27-5-5 "may enter into a transportation contract with the school bus driver under IC 5-22"; IC 20-27-5-6 "may enter into a fleet contract with the fleet contractor under IC 5-22"; IC 20-27-5-7 "Transportation and fleet contracts entered into by a school corporation shall be entered into under IC 5-22"; IC 20-27-5-10(a) public notice "at least ten (10) days before beginning negotiations or receiving proposals or bids"; IC 20-27-5-13 bidder prerequisites; IC 20-27-5-19 sale/assignment requires written agreement of all parties; IC 20-27-5-23 termination without hearing for alcohol; IC 20-27-5-27 equipment buyout at fair market value; IC 20-27-5-28 surety bond. Confirmed sections 8 through 9, 11 through 12, 14 through 18, 20, 22, 24 through 25 are marked Repealed. Confirmed IC 20-35-8-2 IEP transportation text; IC 20-27-12-4 homeless cost-sharing; IC 20-27-12-5 vehicle types. Result: VERIFIED.

(2) IC 5-22-17 chapter PDF (statecodesfiles.justia.com mirror, dated Indiana Code 2016) — fetched via WebFetch, downloaded as PDF, pdftotext extraction. Confirmed VERBATIM IC 5-22-17-3(b) "a contract for supplies may be entered into for a period not to exceed four (4) years" — note language is SUPPLIES, not services; (c) county/municipal-hospital 5-year exception under IC 5-22-7; (d) appropriation contingency; (e) appropriation balance required. Confirmed VERBATIM IC 5-22-17-4(a) escalation index/formula; (b) "with the agreement of the contractor and the purchasing agency, a contract may be renewed any number of times"; (c) renewal term ≤ original term. Confirmed VERBATIM IC 5-22-17-5(a) non-appropriation cancellation; (b) determination "final and conclusive." Cross-verified via WebSearch that 2024 Justia codification of IC 5-22-17-4 returns identical text. Result: VERIFIED.

(3) IC 5-22-6 chapter PDF — fetched via WebFetch, pdftotext extraction. Confirmed VERBATIM Sec. 1 "The purchasing agency of a governmental body may purchase services using any procedure the governmental body or the purchasing agency of the governmental body considers appropriate" and Sec. 2 rules/policies. Result: VERIFIED.

(4) IC 5-22-2 chapter PDF — fetched via WebFetch, pdftotext extraction. Confirmed VERBATIM Sec. 13 "Governmental body" definition including "(4) A political subdivision." Result: VERIFIED.

(5) SBOA Schools portal — re-fetched via WebFetch 2026-06-23. Confirmed page lists under Public Purchasing Law contracts: "Time period for contracts - generally 4 years, IC 5-22-17-3" and "Renewal of contracts, IC 5-22-17-4." Page does not specifically address transportation contracts. Result: VERIFIED (as Stage 1 represented).

(6) IC 20-26-5-4 — confirmed via WebSearch snippet quoting verbatim "(10) Subject to IC 20-27-13, to transport children to and from school, when in the opinion of the governing body the transportation is necessary, including considerations for the safety of the children." Result: VERIFIED.

(7) vLex chapter index — used as path-finder only, confirmed current section headings and Repealed status of sections 8, 9, 11, 12, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 20, 22, 24, 25 in IC 20-27-5. Result: VERIFIED (as cross-reference only).

**Adversarial citation-version conflict.** During the validation pass, WebSearch returned two conflicting paraphrases of IC 20-27-5-7: one quoting current law ("shall be entered into under IC 5-22") and one quoting the old 2006/2011/2012 Justia archived text ("negotiated and let after receiving bids on the basis of specifications…"). Resolved by direct PDF text extraction from the DOE July 1, 2025 booklet, which definitively shows the current text is the IC 5-22 cross-reference with sections 8 through 18 Repealed. The older text is from superseded versions of the chapter.

**Unreachable sources.** (a) Indiana General Assembly (iga.in.gov) section pages — JS-rendered; could not be fetched as text by WebFetch or by direct curl (returns single-page-app HTML shell). Bypassed by using the DOE PDF and Justia chapter PDFs as official-text equivalents. (b) Justia HTML section pages (law.justia.com) — Cloudflare-protected; HTTP 403 to WebFetch and to curl with browser User-Agent. Bypassed by Justia statecodesfiles.justia.com chapter PDFs (which are downloadable mirrors of the codified text). (c) Indiana Register (iar.iga.in.gov) AOA register entries 20190703-IR-010190327AOA and 20120118-IR-010120043AOA — JS-rendered, indexed snippets only, full opinion text not retrieved within the search budget. (d) FindLaw section page — HTTP 403 to WebFetch.

**Current-law check (consolidated).** (1) Official code source: IC 20-27 and IC 5-22 (Indiana Code). (2) Currency: DOE booklet 'Updated July 1, 2025' for IC 20-27; IC 5-22 chapter PDFs dated 2016 with P.L. annotations; confirmed via 2017-2026 session-law sweep that no material amendments to IC 5-22-17-3, 17-4, 17-5, or IC 5-22-6-1 located. (3) Administrative code: 511 IAC (Indiana State Board of Education) and IC 20-27-3 rules of the State School Bus Committee govern vehicle specifications and driver certification, not contracting term/rebid/renewal — none alters the IC 5-22 / IC 20-27-5-7 framework. (4) Both school-specific (IC 20-27-5) and general procurement (IC 5-22) authority checked, with the school-specific statute expressly invoking the general procurement statute via IC 20-27-5-7. (5) Definitions checked: IC 20-27-2 (transportation contract, fleet contract); IC 5-22-2-13 (governmental body — includes political subdivisions = school corporations). (6) SBOA Schools portal, SBOA IASBO 2017 presentation, and AIM Indiana 2021 Purchasing Guide consulted.

https://www.in.gov/sboa/political-subdivisions/schools/ (re-verified — confirmed IC 5-22-17-3 and IC 5-22-17-4 listed as operative for school corporations)

sourcesConsulted:
- IDOE Office of School Transportation, School Transportation Statutes — Updated July 1, 2025 (PDF): https://www.in.gov/doe/files/Statutes-for-Preservice-July-1-2025.pdf — load-bearing primary source; reproduces current IC 20-27 in full, IC 20-35-8, IC 20-40-18, IC 20-50-3.
- IC 5-22-17 chapter PDF (codified text, P.L. annotated): https://statecodesfiles.justia.com/indiana/2016/title-5/article-22/chapter-17/chapter-17.pdf
- IC 5-22-6 chapter PDF: https://statecodesfiles.justia.com/indiana/2016/title-5/article-22/chapter-6/chapter-6.pdf
- IC 5-22-2 chapter PDF: https://statecodesfiles.justia.com/indiana/2016/title-5/article-22/chapter-2/chapter-2.pdf
- IC 20-26-5-4(a)(10): Indiana Code Title 20, Article 26, Chapter 5 — governing body's general transportation authority.
- Indiana State Board of Accounts (SBOA), Schools resource page: https://www.in.gov/sboa/political-subdivisions/schools/ — confirms IC 5-22-17-3 and IC 5-22-17-4 as operative for school corporations.
- SBOA, 'State Purchasing' presentation to IASBO, April 18, 2017: https://www.in.gov/sboa/files/IASBO-Purchasing-presentation-4-18-17.pdf — IC 5-22-6-1 services-procurement framing.
- Accelerate Indiana Municipalities (AIM), 2021 Public Purchasing Guide: https://aimindiana.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/07/2021-Purchasing-Guide.pdf — cross-reference for IC 5-22 supplies/services framework.
- vLex chapter index for IC 20-27-5 (path-finder only): https://us.vlex.com/source/14785/chapter/chapter-5-transportation-contracts-407764711
adversarialVerdictSummary: confirmed (5/6 fields verbatim; District Variance unresolved-unknown as Stage 1 documented); Stage-1 pack survives adversarial review intact; citation-version conflict on IC 20-27-5-7 (old vs. current text) surfaced and resolved in favor of current law
